principles: The human and Pi orchestrator own final decisions; Idu-pi is an advisory supervisor and execution director.
MCP tools inform, audit, recommend, and block unsafe continuation; they do not authorize implementation.
Only a confirmed Project Core and active Constitution can be treated as governance truth.
Evidence beats assertion: plans, tasks, reviews, postflight, and Bibliotecario outputs must expose evidence refs and limitations.
Unknown, missing, stale, or inconsistent context is a blocker for autonomous continuation, not a neutral condition.
Autonomy is bounded and session-directed: automaticov1 proposes, audits, blocks, or creates systemic recommendations; it is not an infinite daemon.
AgentLabs are explicit, sandboxed, audit-only reviewers; they never implement, edit the real repo, commit, push, or promote contracts.
Bibliotecario evidence is a source-intelligence layer, not uncontrolled truth or free web crawling.
forbiddenPractices: Skipping required build, tests, diff check, review, or postflight evidence
Using MCP tools to implement code changes or claim implementation authority
Running automaticov1 as an unbounded daemon
Creating tasks or continuing autonomous execution when readiness is unknown, stale, missing, or blocked
Letting AgentLabs edit the real repository, create commits, push, or implement features
Treating Bibliotecario, community, web, or AI-generated evidence as canonical without corroboration
Promoting contracts, learning rules, or skills without explicit human/orchestrator approval
Installing dependencies, enabling postinstall scripts, publishing, merging, or pushing without explicit approval
Writing runtime state outside configured stateRoot
Hiding failed, irrelevant, missing, or inconsistent evidence instead of surfacing it as a limitation or failure
requiredPractices: Confirm Project Core before activating Constitution gates.
Keep changes inside includedScope and outside excludedScope.
Refresh idu_supervisor_context_pack before delegation or autonomous continuation when context is stale/missing.
Require automaticov1 readiness: confirmed Core, active Constitution, ready task tree, fresh MCP context pack, and no unresolved systemic block.
Use Master Plan → hitos → tasks → subtasks as the execution tree for autonomous proposals.
Record systemic actions when repeated failures, supervisor friction, evidence gaps, security gaps, or DB/schema risks recur.
Use Bibliotecario as bounded evidence: cite source/chunk refs, confidence, limitations, and missing knowledge.
Mark Bibliotecario output as failed or limited when evidence is irrelevant, stale, missing, unsafe, or inconsistent.
Keep MCP outputs advisory-only with explicit decision envelopes and orchestratorDecisionRequired for action.
Run build/tests/diff-check and fresh review for non-trivial changes before commit.
Ask human approval before push, merge, release, dependency changes, contract promotion, skill installation, or AgentLab execution.

preferredStack: Telegram as optional remote interface only

rejectedStack.summary: Unbounded autonomous daemons — long-running processes without shutdown wiring
rejectedStack.rationale: Catches setInterval/setTimeout/cron/while(true) patterns WITHOUT a SIGTERM/SIGINT/process.exit/clearInterval cleanup handler. LLM-discretion clause: orchestrator may flag additional patterns as 'unbounded' (e.g. while-true loops without a stop condition, dynamic tick schedulers). behaviorPattern is advisory-grade — false negatives possible if cleanup logic lives in a comment or string literal.

messages.blocked: Rechazado por Project Core (item 1): proceso long-running sin manejo de shutdown. Detalle en rationale.
messages.warning: Posible rechazo (advisory, item 1, long-running): proceso sin SIGTERM/SIGINT/clearInterval visible.

rejectedStack.summary: Unbounded autonomous daemons — periodic setInterval schedulers
rejectedStack.rationale: Catches setInterval( calls regardless of shutdown wiring. LLM-discretion clause: orchestrator may flag additional patterns as 'unbounded' (e.g. while-true loops without a stop condition, dynamic tick schedulers). behaviorPattern is advisory-grade — false negatives possible if cleanup logic lives in a comment or string literal.

messages.blocked: Rechazado por Project Core (item 1): setInterval detectado sin evidencia de cleanup.
messages.warning: Posible rechazo (advisory, item 1, periodic): setInterval visible — verificar shutdown wiring.

rejectedStack.summary: MCP tools that implement code or authorize changes — MCP server entrypoint
rejectedStack.rationale: Targets the MCP server entrypoint src/mcp-server.ts (verified on disk: 120,526 bytes). This file registers all MCP tools via the server.tool(...) calls; any change here can implement new code-authoring tools or authorize new authority surfaces. LLM-discretion clause: orchestrator may whitelist legitimate uses of writeFile/execSync/spawnSync inside the MCP write surface (e.g. lab artifact paths, migration runners), flag additional entrypoint changes beyond src/mcp-server.ts / src/cli.ts, or flag handler changes outside src/mcp/** that add non-advisory tools.

messages.blocked: Rechazado por Project Core (item 2): cambio en src/mcp-server.ts requiere human confirmation.

rejectedStack.rationale: Targets the CLI entrypoint src/cli.ts (verified on disk: 80,394 bytes), which can be invoked to write files, run migrations, or trigger side-effects. LLM-discretion clause: orchestrator may whitelist legitimate uses of writeFile/execSync/spawnSync inside the MCP write surface (e.g. lab artifact paths, migration runners), flag additional entrypoint changes beyond src/mcp-server.ts / src/cli.ts, or flag handler changes outside src/mcp/** that add non-advisory tools.

messages.blocked: Rechazado por Project Core (item 2): cambio en src/cli.ts requiere human confirmation.

rejectedStack.summary: AgentLabs that edit the real repository — src/agentlab-*.ts file surface
rejectedStack.rationale: All AgentLabs code lives at src/agentlab-*.ts (6 files: agentlab-contract.ts, agentlab-effectiveness-events.ts, agentlab-report-consolidation.ts, agentlab-review-requests.ts, agentlab-review-runner.ts, agentlab-supervisor-contract.ts). The agentlabs/** path does NOT exist in this repo — this glob is the actual file surface. Every change to these files is a deterministic write to the real repository.

rejectedStack.summary: AgentLabs that edit the real repository — shell-exec / file-write imports
rejectedStack.rationale: Catches execSync/spawnSync/writeFile imports inside any agentlabs file. Fully deterministic — these primitives are the write surface of AgentLabs and cannot be allowed in audit-only contexts.

messages.blocked: Rechazado por Project Core (item 3): execSync/spawnSync/writeFile visible bajo src/agentlab-*.ts.

rejectedStack.summary: AgentLabs that edit the real repository — git commit/push commands
rejectedStack.rationale: Catches `git commit` or `git push` lines inside any diff body. Word boundaries prevent substring false positives. Fully deterministic — these commands are the irreversible surface of AgentLabs and cannot be allowed.

messages.blocked: Rechazado por Project Core (item 3): git commit/push detectado en diff.
messages.warning: Posible rechazo (advisory, item 3): git commit/push visible.

rejectedStack.rationale: Catches curl/wget/http.get/fetch invocations that hit news/rss/blog/medium/reddit endpoints — the common shape of uncontrolled web search. LLM-discretion clause: orchestrator may whitelist legitimate use of cheerio/puppeteer/playwright (e.g. consented tests, agentlab review sandboxes) or flag additional fetch patterns not covered by the commandPattern (e.g. axios.get, undici).

messages.blocked: Rechazado por Project Core (item 4): import de cheerio/puppeteer/playwright detectado.

rejectedStack.rationale: Catches postinstall / preinstall hooks in diff content. These are reserved npm lifecycle scripts that execute at install time. Advisory-grade (text-fragility clause): commandPattern matches against git diff body; false positives possible if a comment or string literal mentions postinstall/preinstall. The repo's REAL protection is repo-level config — pnpm-workspace.yaml has ignoreScripts: true and .npmrc has ignore-scripts=true — so this rule is a residual textual signal, not the authoritative check. Orchestrator clause: orchestrator may whitelist legitimate uses (e.g. a documented opt-in for a specific dev dependency) and may flag additional hook names (e.g. preprepare).

messages.blocked: Rechazado por Project Core (item 5): hook postinstall/preinstall detectado.
messages.warning: Posible rechazo (advisory, item 5): hook postinstall visible.

rejectedStack: Repo writes outside explicit worker/orchestrator flows

securityRules: Auth, secrets, permissions, MCP authority, dependency changes, and external-source handling require explicit review.
Prompt-injection, RAG/source poisoning, provenance, ACL-before-retrieval, and least-privilege risks must be surfaced for Bibliotecario/RAG-like flows.
MCP runtime freshness must be checked after src/mcp-server.ts or build changes; reload is explicit and user/orchestrator controlled.
Do not enable dependency scripts globally; dependency/build exceptions must be explicit and reviewable.
dataRules: Runtime reports, usage events, source indexes, lab DB, and project state must stay under stateRoot for enrolled projects.
Do not store raw prompts, full docs, secrets, tokens, or unnecessary sensitive data in context-quality or usage telemetry.
DB/schema/storage changes require a plan, rollback/migration reasoning, tests, and security review.
Source Library/Bibliotecario may expose bounded source refs and chunks, not promote raw source content into contracts automatically.
approvalRules: Project Core must be confirmed before this Constitution can become active.
automaticov1 may not proceed unless execution readiness is satisfied and systemic maintenance is clear.
High/blocker requests require human/orchestrator confirmation before implementation.
Push, merge, release, dependency updates, contract promotion, skill installation, and AgentLab execution require explicit approval.
Changes outside preferredStack or inside excludedScope require human decision and may be blocked.
Fresh reviewer is required before commit/push for non-trivial code changes.

validationGates.description: Project Core must be confirmed before governance gates are active.
Constitution must be active before autonomous continuation.
automaticov1 must block on missing/unknown/stale readiness.
Stale or missing MCP supervisor context pack blocks autonomous continuation.
Master Plan task tree must be ready before automatic execution proposals.
Repeated failures, supervisor friction, or evidence/security gaps require systemic action before continuing.
MCP must remain advisory-only and must not implement or authorize work.
AgentLabs must remain explicit, sandboxed, audit-only, and non-implementing.
Missing, irrelevant, inconsistent, unsafe, or uncorroborated evidence must be surfaced as limitation/failure.
DB/schema/storage changes require plan, rollback/migration reasoning, tests, and security review.
Auth/login/security/secret changes require confirmation and security review.
Excluded scope must not be implemented.
Rejected stack or rejected architecture patterns are blocked.
Non-preferred stack or architecture changes require confirmation.
Medium/high/critical data handling changes require security/data review.
